phrases.intent_en: Ask how to get to the library
phrases.text_local: Pardon, comment puis-je aller à la bibliothèque ?
phrases.pronunciation: par-DON, koh-MON pwee-juh ah-LAY ah lah beeb-lee-oh-TEK
phrases.cultural_note: University libraries ('bibliothèques universitaires') in France are central to student life. The word 'bibliothèque' — long but essential — is worth learning. UK's William T. Young Library is one of the largest academic libraries in the South.

responses.french: Allez tout droit et tournez à gauche après le bâtiment principal.
responses.english: Go straight ahead and turn left after the main building.
responses.phonetic: ah-LAY too DRWAH ay toor-NAY ah GOHSH ah-PRAY luh bah-tee-MON pran-see-PAL

responses.french: La bibliothèque est à dix minutes à pied.
responses.english: The library is ten minutes on foot.
responses.phonetic: lah beeb-lee-oh-TEK ay tah dee mee-NOOT ah PYAY

phrases.text_local: Je visite le campus aujourd'hui. C'est très impressionnant.
phrases.pronunciation: juh vee-ZEET luh KOM-poos oh-zhoor-DWEE. say tray an-press-yo-NON
phrases.cultural_note: French universities ('universités') don't typically have American-style campuses — they tend to be integrated into city fabric. Visiting a large American campus like UK is often genuinely striking for French visitors.

phrases.text_local: Excusez-moi, où se trouve le département de français ?
phrases.pronunciation: ex-kyoo-ZAY-mwah, oo suh TROOV luh day-par-tuh-MON duh fron-SAY
phrases.cultural_note: UK's Department of Modern and Classical Languages, Literatures, and Cultures is home to French studies. Asking for the French department on a US campus in French is a fun, self-referential moment.

responses.french: Le département de français se trouve dans le bâtiment des langues modernes.
responses.english: The French department is in the modern languages building.
responses.phonetic: luh day-par-tuh-MON duh fron-SAY suh TROOV don luh bah-tee-MON day LONG mo-DERN

phrases.intent_en: Comment that the campus is beautiful
phrases.text_local: Ce campus est magnifique, surtout au printemps !
phrases.pronunciation: suh KOM-poos ay mah-nyee-FEEK, soor-TOO oh pran-TON
phrases.cultural_note: UK's campus is known for its dogwood and redbud trees in spring. French universities are often housed in historic urban buildings — the green, open American campus style genuinely impresses French visitors.

phrases.intent_en: Say you'd like to study French here
phrases.text_local: J'aimerais étudier le français ici un jour.
phrases.pronunciation: jhem-RAY ay-too-DYAY luh fron-SAY ee-SEE un ZHOOR
phrases.cultural_note: 'J'aimerais' (I would like / I would love to) expresses a warm, genuine wish. Using it about learning French creates a charming moment — especially if you're already speaking French to say it.

responses.french: C'est une excellente idée ! Le programme de français est très réputé.
responses.english: That's an excellent idea! The French program is very well regarded.
responses.phonetic: say toon ex-el-ONT ee-DAY ! luh pro-GRAM duh fron-SAY ay tray ray-poo-TAY
